rulings.Case Title: JEFFREY BONHACH VS. MALIBU PIER PARTNERS LLC A DELAWARE PARTNERSHIP et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, Arpil 3, 2009, line 7, CROSS DEFENDANT JEFFREY BONHACH'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend. Causes of action, not time barred. Cross-complainant to plead breach of contract with greater specificity as to terms of the agreement and facts constituting breach. Facts must be alleged regarding existence of confidential relationship, that a communication was made in confidence and disclosed, and nature of duty of undivided loyalty. =(302CWW/JW)

rulings.Case Title: FRANCIS DAVID RYAN VS. GUNTHER DERTZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: Re-Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsieration To Third Amended Complaintq
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, Arpil 3, 2009, line 10, PLAINTIFF FRANCIS RYAN'S Re-Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsieration To Third Amended Complaint. Denied. Court lacks jurisdiction to hear this motion because judgment was entered on March 17, 2009. See Ramon vs. Aerospace Corp., (1996) 50 Cal.App. 4th, 1233, 1237-1238; APRI Ins. Co. vs. Sup. Crt. (1999), 76 Cal.App. 4th, 176, 181. =(302CWW)

rulings.Case Title: JOE GROSSMAN VS. ISIDRO SAENZPARDO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Motion To Rescind Settlement Agreements
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, Arpil 3, 2009, line 20, DEFENDANT ISIDRO SAENZPARDO, JUAN SAENZPARDO, and, MARIA SAENZPARDO'S Motion To Rescind Settlement Agreements. Motion to rescind is denied. =(302CWW/PB)

rulings.Case Title: JOE GROSSMAN VS. ISIDRO SAENZPARDO et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, Arpil 3, 2009, line 20, PLAINTIFF JOE GROSSMAN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/AND OR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Motion for summary judgment/summary adjudication is denied. Even though the master tenant forfeitetd the lease, there is a triable issue of fact whether the remaining subtenants are "tenants" under SFAC 37.2(t) as subtenants approved by the landlord. See ParkMerced Co. vs. S.F. Rent Stabilization and Arbitration Board, 215 Cal.App. 3d, 490; DeZerega vs. Meggs, 83 Cal.App. 4th, 28. Defendants raise a triable issue of fact whether a nuisance existed, gouging occured, approval of the subtenancies (waiver/estoppel), and whether more than ten persons resided at the premises at the same time. =(302CWW/PB)

rulings.Case Title: GEORGE DAVID VS. TODD MCCULLOUGH et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, Arpil 3, 2009, line 21, PLAINTIFF GEORGE DAVID'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant raises a Triable Issue of Fact regarding whether owner failed to accommodate a disability request. =(302CWW)

rulings.Case Title: VICTOR POPOFF VS. ISTVAN TALAS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Be Declared Prevailing Party And For Reasonable Attorney'S Fees And Costs
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, Arpil 3, 2009, line 19, DEFENDANT ISTVAN TALAS, and, GIZELLA ELEK'S Motion To Be Declared Prevailing Party And For Reasonable Attorney's Fees And Costs. Defendants are prevailing parties under CCP section 1032(a)(4). Defendants are awarded reasonable attorney's fees pursuant to section 37.10B(b)(6) of the San Francisco Administrative Code in the reasonable amount of $3,975, plus costs of $240. Billing rate reasonable and 13.25 hours reasonable and necessary for defense and recovery of attorneys fees. =(302CWW/JW)
